analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

operators: layer::unique::prologue (1 fused node, 12 SGEMM kernel launches across 3 dedup iterations)

bottleneck.description: Every matrix multiply in the workload dispatches to the SIMT FP32 scalar path. The kernel name cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x256_8x4_tn_align1 encodes this directly: _simt_ means cuBLAS selected the scalar multiply-accumulate SIMT pipeline, not Tensor Cores. smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active.avg.pct_of_peak_sustained_active is 0.0 across all 20 SGEMM launches, confirmed by tensor_core_active_pct: 0.0, meaning every Blackwell Tensor Core is idle for 66.6% of the attributed trace time. A secondary symptom: 210 registers per thread multiplied by 256 threads per block equals 53,760 registers per block. With a Blackwell SM register file of 65,536 registers, only 1 block fits per SM, yielding ~16.5% achieved occupancy. This starves the warp scheduler of warps to switch between, leaving 42% of cycles without an eligible issue candidate (eligible_cycles_pct ~58%). Blackwell native Tensor Core kernels use fewer registers per thread, raising occupancy and enabling latency hiding. On Blackwell (Sm100) with BF16, Tensor Core peak throughput is roughly 10-16x SIMT FP32 at the hardware level; practical GEMM speedup after tile-efficiency and memory effects is 4-8x for these matrix shapes.

transformation.description: Insert aten._to_copy casts to bfloat16 on both operands of every aten.mm.default node, and cast the output back to float32 to preserve downstream dtype contracts. At execution time this routes cuBLAS from the SIMT SGEMM path (cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x256_8x4_tn_align1) to a Tensor Core GEMM path (e.g. cutlass_80_tensorop_bf16_*). The preferred inference approach is model.bfloat16() which avoids the round-trip casts entirely; the aten-level FX pass is for cases where only the GEMM precision can be changed without altering the module dtype.
transformation.location: Aten-level FX graph: every aten.mm.default node whose first operand has dtype == float32.
fx_steps: if node.op != 'call_function' or node.target is not torch.ops.aten.mm.default:
transformation.code_hint:
# Fastest path for inference-only: no FX surgery required.
# model.bfloat16()
# input_tensor = input_tensor.bfloat16()
# model_compiled = torch.compile(model, backend='inductor')
# cuBLAS automatically selects the BF16 Tensor Core path.
#
# Zero-precision-change alternative:
# torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True  # before torch.compile
# Keeps FP32 inputs, uses TF32 Tensor Core accumulation: ~3-4x over SIMT FP32.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Minimal DRAM change — L2 hit rate is 91.5% and weights are effectively L2-resident. Primary gain is compute throughput: BF16 Tensor Core replaces SIMT FP32. Lower-register Tensor Core kernel also raises occupancy, reducing eligible_cycles_pct from ~58% toward ~80-90%.

optimizations.notes: Assumes inference-only (no autograd). The allow_tf32 alternative keeps FP32 accumulation semantics and is safer for production when BF16 numerical change is unacceptable. The cast-around-mm FX pass adds two _to_copy nodes per mm; Inductor CSE will eliminate duplicate casts of the same weight node across the graph. This pass must also cover aten.addmm.default if biases are present (not observed in this profile).

operators: aten::mm op_id=4 (Q), op_id=5 (K), op_id=6 (V) — 3 nodes sharing same activation input, attention layer A
aten::mm op_id=26 (Q), op_id=27 (K), op_id=28 (V) — 3 nodes sharing same activation input, attention layer B
layer::unique::prologue (3 iterations x Q/K/V triplet = 9 SGEMM launches for Q/K/V)

bottleneck.description: Q, K, and V projections are three independent F.linear calls that all read the same input activation tensor [4096, 512] = 8 MB. Each launches a separate cutlass SGEMM kernel sequentially. The activation is read from L2 three times per attention layer (L2 hit rate 91.5% confirms it stays warm). Fusing them into a single [4096x512] x [512x1536] GEMM loads the activation once and produces Q+K+V in a single kernel launch, reducing L2 activation reads by 2/3 and eliminating two kernel launches per attention layer. The current per-projection SGEMM grid is [64,1,2]=128 CTAs; on the estimated ~170 SM Blackwell device this is a single partial wave at ~75% SM utilization. The fused grid [64,3,2]=384 CTAs spans ~3 waves, maintaining SM utilization across more work.

transformation.description: At the functional (pre-AOTAutograd decomposition) IR level, detect triples of F.linear calls whose first argument is the same FX node (the shared activation). Stack the three weight parameters along dim=0 into a single [3*out_dim, in_dim] weight, run one fused F.linear, then slice the [batch, seq, 3*out_dim] output back into Q, K, V tensors. At functional level the shared activation is a single FX node, making the pattern unambiguous. After decomposition the single fused call lowers to one wide aten.mm, eliminating 2 of 3 kernel launches per attention layer.
transformation.location: Dynamo FX graph (pre-AOTAutograd decomposition): F.linear / aten.linear.default call nodes grouped by their shared first-argument node.
fx_steps: out_dim = 512  # model.head_dim * model.num_heads; parameterize from config
transformation.code_hint:
# Preferred: implement at module level to avoid runtime cat overhead.
# self.qkv_weight = nn.Parameter(torch.cat([W_q, W_k, W_v], dim=0))  # shape [3*d, d]
# def forward(self, x):
#     qkv = F.linear(x, self.qkv_weight)  # single wide GEMM
#     q, k, v = qkv.chunk(3, dim=-1)
# Register the stacked weight before torch.compile so Inductor sees a single constant.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Activation [4096,512]=8MB read once per attention layer instead of three times, saving ~16MB L2 reads per attention layer. Across 5 attention-layer sets in this trace: ~80MB L2 read reduction.

optimizations.notes: Must be applied at functional level before AOTAutograd decomposition. After decomposition each aten.mm has its own aten.view of the activation and the shared-input identity is lost. The out_dim slice boundary (512) is hardcoded to this model's projection size; parameterize via the nn.Linear.out_features attribute of the matched modules. If any of the three F.linear calls has a bias, cat the biases along dim=0 and pass as the third argument to the fused call.

operators: aten::_efficient_attention_forward op_id=14 (1 node, fused with aten::_scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention op_id=7)
aten::_efficient_attention_forward op_id=36 (1 node, fused with aten::_scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention op_id=29)

bottleneck.description: SDPA dispatches to the xFormers memory-efficient attention backend, launching fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_64x64_rf_sm80 on every call. The kernel name carries two problems. First, f32: the attention computation runs in FP32, precluding Tensor Core acceleration inside the softmax-V pipeline. Second, sm80: this is a CUTLASS kernel compiled for Sm80 (Ampere) running on Sm100 (Blackwell) via backward compatibility. ncu reports metrics.raw: {} (empty object) for all five fmha launches, which is the direct symptom of the Sm80/Sm100 ISA mismatch preventing hardware counter collection. Running Sm80 SASS on Sm100 foregoes all Blackwell-specific attention optimizations: the async warpgroup MMA pipeline, increased SMEM capacity per SM, and native warp-specialized kernels. PyTorch 2.11+cu128 ships Flash Attention 2 kernels with native Sm100 SASS. Switching the SDPA backend before compilation replaces the Sm80 binary with a native Sm100 kernel, restoring ncu counter coverage and, with BF16 inputs from OPT-1 (applied at aten level), enables Tensor Core acceleration inside the attention kernel.

transformation.description: Before torch.compile, set Flash Attention as the active SDPA backend so Dynamo traces F.scaled_dot_product_attention to aten._scaled_dot_product_flash_attention (Sm100-native kernel) rather than aten._scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention (Sm80 xFormers kernel). Combined with BF16 inputs (OPT-1, applied at aten level which runs after this functional pass), the FA kernel engages the Tensor Core MMA pipeline. Option B provides an aten-level graph surgery fallback for environments where pre-compilation flags cannot be set.
transformation.location: Pre-compilation SDPA backend flags (Option A, preferred) or aten-level FX surgery on aten._scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention.default nodes (Option B).
fx_steps: # Option A — pre-compilation (zero graph surgery, preferred):
# Dynamo now traces SDPA to aten._scaled_dot_product_flash_attention.
q, k, v = node.args[0], node.args[1], node.args[2]
# Signature: (q, k, v, attn_bias, compute_log_sumexp, dropout_p, is_causal, scale)
is_causal  = node.args[6] if len(node.args) > 6 else False
scale      = node.args[7] if len(node.args) > 7 else None
transformation.code_hint:
# Verify backend selection at runtime before benchmarking:
# from torch.nn.attention import SDPBackend, sdpa_kernel
# with sdpa_kernel([SDPBackend.FLASH_ATTENTION]):
#     out = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v)
#
# Even without BF16 (before OPT-1 is applied), Option A provides immediate benefit:
# it replaces the Sm80 xFormers binary with a native Sm100 FA2 FP32 kernel,
# resolving the ISA mismatch and restoring ncu counter coverage.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Flash Attention fuses the QK^T matmul, row-wise softmax, and softmax*V matmul into a single tiled HBM sweep, eliminating the full intermediate attention score matrix. For this workload (batch=8, heads=8, seq=512, head_dim=64) the intermediate matrix is 8*8*512*512*4 bytes = 67 MB per forward pass — fully eliminated by FA kernel tiling.

optimizations.notes: OPT-1 (aten level) runs after this functional-level pass by pipeline level ordering, so BF16 inputs will be in place at execution time. Option B aten-pass argument indexing is based on the PyTorch 2.11 aten schema for _scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention and should be validated against the live schema via torch.ops.aten._scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention.default._schema before use. If the model uses causal masking (is_causal=True), Flash Attention handles it natively with no extra mask tensor.

operators: All aten::mm weight parameter nodes (20 weight tensors across 9 operator sites)

bottleneck.description: Weight parameters are not marked as compile-time constants. Without freezing, Inductor emits guards for weight shape, stride, and dtype at every forward call, and performs runtime aten.t() or aten.view() to produce the transposed-normal layout (_tn_) that cuBLAS expects for mm(activation, weight). The _tn_ suffix in cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x256_8x4_tn_align1 encodes this: the left matrix is transposed at dispatch time. With freezing enabled, Inductor hoists the transpose to compile time, pre-packs the weight into the layout cuBLAS prefers, and eliminates the per-call guard overhead. This is an incremental gain on top of OPT-1 but is zero-risk for inference workloads.

transformation.description: Set freezing=True in torch.compile options so Inductor treats all nn.Parameter tensors with requires_grad=False as compile-time constants. Inductor folds them into kernel launch parameters, emits pre-transposed weight layouts eliminating the runtime _tn_ transpose, and removes per-call weight guards. Adding max_autotune=True allows Inductor to benchmark cuBLAS and Triton GEMM tile configurations against the frozen layouts and select the fastest variant rather than using heuristic defaults.
transformation.location: torch.compile options dict, applied during Inductor lowering.
fx_steps: # Or via global config before compile:
transformation.code_hint:
# Requires model.eval() — freezing is incompatible with training.
# First compiled call triggers autotune benchmarking (slow);
# cache persists for subsequent calls in the same process.
# To persist autotune cache across runs:
# torch._inductor.config.autotune_local_cache = True
# After OPT-1 switches to BF16 Tensor Core kernels, freezing additionally
# enables Inductor to emit the pre-packed BF16 weight format for maximum TC tile reuse.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates runtime aten.t() / view() calls and weight guard checks per GEMM. After OPT-1, allows Inductor to emit pre-packed BF16 weight layout maximizing Tensor Core tile reuse.

optimizations.notes: OPT-1 is listed as a prerequisite because freezing is most impactful after BF16 dtype promotion: BF16 Tensor Core kernels expose more layout-sensitive tuning knobs than SIMT SGEMM, and max_autotune can discover 5-15% additional improvement over heuristic defaults in the TC regime. Impact estimate is conservative and workload-specific; actual gain depends on the tile configuration autotuned by Inductor. Freezing is incompatible with torch.compile(dynamic=True) when weight shapes vary across calls.

global_notes: 2 operators below 1% threshold omitted (combined 1.12% of attributed time): aten::native_layer_norm_0 (0.50%, 9088 ns, 2 kernels) and aten::_unsafe_view_0 (0.62%, 11328 ns, 2 kernels).

hardware-limits.md not found on disk. Device NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition (Sm100 / GB202) properties inferred from published Blackwell architecture: estimated ~170 SMs at 128 FP32 CUDA cores per SM. Wave calculations use 170 SMs as reference value; if the actual SM count differs, revisit wave efficiency estimates for OPT-2.

warp_cycles_per_instruction is null for all operators — this counter was removed in Blackwell (Sm100). Latency-bound proxy applied: eligible_cycles_pct < 20% indicates latency-bound. All SGEMM operators report eligible_cycles_pct ~58%, placing them in the compute-throughput-bound regime consistent with SIMT FP32 arithmetic saturation at low (~16.5%) occupancy, not latency-bound.

fused_kernel_double_count flag active: layer::unique::prologue_0 (is_fused=true, contains aten::native_layer_norm + aten::_unsafe_view + aten::add plus all 21 attention kernels), aten::_efficient_attention_forward op_id=14 and op_id=36 (each is_fused=true, fused with _scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention), aten::_unsafe_view_0 (is_fused=true). The time budget lists fused operators as single line-items; their constituent kernel durations are not double-counted against the granular aten::mm and aten::native_layer_norm entries.

Layer deduplication active: layer::unique::prologue_0 encapsulates 3 identical attention layers in one NVTX range (21 kernels = 3 iterations x 7 kernels per iter: pre-LN + 3x Q/K/V SGEMM + FMHA + output SGEMM + post-LN). The individual aten::mm operators (op_id=4,5,6,21 and op_id=26,27,28,43) represent 2 additional attention layers profiled as separate NVTX ranges. Total attention layers in workload: 5.

SGEMM dominates at 66.6% of total attributed time (1,219,411 ns across 20 launches). FMHA is second at 30.6% (559,451 ns across 5 launches). LayerNorm Triton kernels account for 2.8% (51,040 ns) — below actionable threshold.

fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_64x64_rf_sm80 reports empty ncu metrics (metrics.raw: {}) for all 5 launches due to Sm80/Sm100 ISA mismatch as flagged in the capture context note. Duration-based impact estimates for OPT-3 are relative comparisons only; actual speedup from switching to a native Sm100 FA kernel in production depends on the specific FA2 variant selected by PyTorch 2.11 on this Blackwell device.

total_attributed_ns = 1,830,902 ns (sum of all 13 operator aggregated.total_duration_ns values). total_profiled_wall_time_ms = 2.523 ms derived from first kernel start (k_00009, 3903691683 ns) to last kernel end (k_00043, 3906214804 ns). The gap between attributed time and wall span is explained by non-kernel CPU scheduling and NVTX overhead.

Recommended application order by pipeline level: (1) OPT-2 at functional level during Dynamo capture; (2) OPT-3 at functional level during Dynamo capture, after OPT-2 has formed QKV output nodes; (3) OPT-1 at aten level during AOTAutograd lowering; (4) OPT-4 at inductor_config level during Inductor lowering. Within aten level, OPT-1 BF16 promotion must run before OPT-3 Option B because Flash Attention dispatch at runtime checks input dtype. Cross-level ordering (functional before aten before inductor_config) is enforced by the backend pipeline and does not require explicit prerequisite encoding.

duration_ns values are 2-5x inflated by ncu counter-collection application-replay overhead. All estimated_impact.relative_duration_reduction_ns values are relative comparisons within this profile and must not be used as absolute wall-clock latency predictions.
